speaker: alice
text: Right then, time for our green segment and we're starting with some absolutely brilliant conservation news from Central Australia. <cite index='17-14,17-15'>Threatened Golden Bandicoot numbers have tripled inside a feral predator-free area, showing just how quickly native species can bounce back when feral cats and foxes are removed from the landscape</cite>. This is exactly the kind of success story we love hearing about.

speaker: charlie
text: That's fantastic news Jamie, proving that targeted conservation really works when you give our native animals a fighting chance. And speaking of environmental wins, <cite index='11-3,11-4'>Australia's Nature Positive Plan has moved from policy intent to active implementation, with the most significant overhaul of environment protection laws since 1999 now rolling out</cite>. This includes that crucial shift from 'no net loss' to actually requiring environmental benefit.

speaker: alice
text: <cite index='11-17'>Fossil fuel developments can no longer use the National Interest fast-track exemption, significantly tightening approval pathways for new coal and gas projects</cite>. Plus there's enhanced Indigenous knowledge integration and stronger protections for threatened species. It's proper environmental reform that's been years in the making, and it's finally happening.
